description:
You are the site administrator for a small web application.  
    A data directory already exists at  
        /home/user/site_admin_data  

    Inside this directory you will find two files:

      1. /home/user/site_admin_data/users.json  
         • An array that currently lists 5 user-account objects.  
      2. /home/user/site_admin_data/user_schema.json  
         • A JSON-Schema (draft-07) describing what a *valid* user object
           must look like.  The schema enforces:
             - id        : integer
             - username  : string
             - active    : boolean
             - roles     : array of strings
           No additional properties are allowed.

    Unfortunately some user objects in users.json are out of compliance
    with the schema.  Your job is to perform an “account audit” using only
    standard command-line tools that are already available in the basic
    Linux container (e.g. jq, grep, awk, sed, python3, etc.).

    Required end-state:

    1. Leave the original two source files completely **unchanged**.  
    2. Create two *new* files, **in the same directory**, with the following
       exact names and contents:

       a) /home/user/site_admin_data/valid_users.json  
          • A single-line, *minified* JSON array that contains every user
            object that fully conforms to user_schema.json.  
          • The array must preserve the original object ordering found in
            users.json.  
          • No trailing newline is allowed after the closing bracket.

       b) /home/user/site_admin_data/invalid_users.log  
          • A plain-text log.  One row per invalid user, in the same
            order they appear in users.json.  
          • Use the **exact** pipe-delimited format shown below
            (no whitespace around pipes):

                id:<ID>|username:<USERNAME>|errors:<ERROR-LIST>

            <ID>         : the integer value stored in the user object.  
            <USERNAME>   : the username string if it exists; otherwise *null*.  
            <ERROR-LIST> : comma-separated, lowercase tokens that diagnose
                           why the object is invalid, in this priority
                           order (include all that apply):
                               - missing_id_field
                               - id_wrong_type
                               - missing_username_field
                               - username_wrong_type
                               - missing_active_field
                               - active_wrong_type
                               - missing_roles_field
                               - roles_wrong_type
                               - additional_props_present

          • Example of two possible lines (these are **examples only**):
                id:42|username:null|errors:missing_username_field
                id:7|username:bob|errors:active_wrong_type,additional_props_present

          • The log must end with a single trailing newline.

    3. When you are done, the only *new* filesystem artefacts should be the
       two files described above; no temp files should remain.

    Hints & constraints:

      • Use jq’s type, has, and length filters (or any other standard
        command-line tool) to inspect each object.  
      • Remember that the schema disallows additional properties.  
      • Make sure the audit handles the exact five objects already in
        users.json; do **not** assume there will be any more or any fewer.  
      • You do NOT need to install any external libraries; use what is
        pre-installed.  
      • Be careful to produce *bit-for-bit* accurate output, because the
        automated checker will compare both files for an exact match.

    Once the two required files exist and match the above specification,
    the task is complete.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the automated test-suite.
        *** DO NOT reveal anything inside <truth> to the agent. ***

        ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        INITIAL STATE (pre-existing files)
        ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    -->

    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/site_admin_data/users.json"><![CDATA[
[
  { "id": 1, "username": "alice", "active": true,  "roles": ["admin", "editor"] },
  { "id": 2, "username": "bob",   "roles": ["editor"] },
  { "id": 3, "active": false,     "roles": ["viewer"] },
  { "id": 4, "username": "carol", "active": "yes",  "roles": ["editor"] },
  { "id": 5, "username": "dan",   "active": false, "roles": ["viewer", "commenter"] }
]
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/site_admin_data/user_schema.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["id", "username", "active", "roles"],
  "properties": {
    "id":       { "type": "integer" },
    "username": { "type": "string"  },
    "active":   { "type": "boolean" },
    "roles": {
      "type": "array",
      "items": { "type": "string" }
    }
  },
  "additionalProperties": false
}
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <!--
        ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        EXPECTED END-STATE (files to be created by the agent)
        ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    -->
    <expected_files>
        <file path="/home/user/site_admin_data/valid_users.json"><![CDATA[[{"id":1,"username":"alice","active":true,"roles":["admin","editor"]},{"id":5,"username":"dan","active":false,"roles":["viewer","commenter"]}]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/site_admin_data/invalid_users.log"><![CDATA[
id:2|username:bob|errors:missing_active_field
id:3|username:null|errors:missing_username_field
id:4|username:carol|errors:active_wrong_type
]]></file>
    </expected_files>
